preview_text:
The model favors Brazil with a 64% win probability, leaving Scotland at 12% and a draw at 25%. Brazil enters as the heavy favorite, though the model assigns Scotland a modest but non-negligible upset chance rather than treating the outcome as settled.

The 64–12 split indicates a pronounced gap in expected quality, yet the 25% draw probability suggests the match retains genuine competitive texture. A Scotland victory remains unlikely under the model, but a stalemate is plausible enough to prevent Brazil from overwhelming the odds—roughly one in four times this fixture runs, a draw materializes.

The model and sportsbooks align closely. The consensus across 32 books puts Brazil at 65%, nearly identical to the model's 64%, and Scotland at 15% versus the model's 12%—a gap of 3 percentage points. Polymarket prices diverge slightly further, crediting Scotland to 16% and Brazil to 66%, but all three sources agree on the fundamental shape: a Brazil favorite in a match where draws are neither rare nor improbable. The dashboard makes no claim about which forecast is most accurate.
